Risk Assessment: 9 Major interactions including life-threatening bleeding and cardiac risks; elderly patient with CKD 3a; 13 concurrent medications; multiple prescribers (5) increasing fragmentation risk.
CYP2C9/3A4 inhibition by amiodarone causes markedly elevated INR. This patient is on both chronically — highest bleeding risk interaction. Effect persists for weeks even after discontinuation.
Dual QT prolongation risk. Combined use increases risk of Torsades de Pointes and sudden cardiac death. This 71-year-old male with cardiac disease is at elevated baseline risk.
Triple antithrombotic/bleeding risk: warfarin (anticoagulant) + aspirin (antiplatelet) + sertraline (impaired platelet aggregation). Synergistic GI and intracranial hemorrhage risk is very high.
Additive AV nodal suppression. Risk of severe bradycardia, heart block, and cardiac arrest. Heart rate and rhythm require close monitoring.
CYP3A4 inhibition increases statin levels. Risk of rhabdomyolysis — particularly concerning given CKD 3a (impaired CK/myoglobin clearance).
At 650 mg Q6H PRN, maximum daily dose reaches 2600 mg — exceeding the 2 g/day threshold for INR elevation. Regular use is contraindicated without INR monitoring.

On therapy since 2019 (>5 years). Long-term PPI use increases fracture risk (compounded by impaired calcium absorption), hypomagnesemia risk, and CKD progression. Interacts with warfarin (CYP2C19) and calcium supplement.
Trial step-down to H2-blocker (famotidine) or switch to pantoprazole if PPI required (less CYP interaction). Reassess GERD symptoms.
Multiple Major interactions: QT prolongation with amiodarone (life-threatening), bleeding risk with warfarin and aspirin. Indication is mild depression only.
Consider non-interacting antidepressant: bupropion or mirtazapine (no QT prolongation, no platelet/CYP effects). Reassess depression severity.
In setting of therapeutic warfarin for AFib stroke prevention, added aspirin confers marginal benefit but significantly increases hemorrhagic risk (especially with concurrent sertraline).
Reassess indication: if CHA₂DS₂-VASc score adequately managed by warfarin alone, consider discontinuing aspirin. ACC/AHA guidelines suggest avoiding dual therapy unless recent ACS/stent.
Contributes to fall risk via sedation/dizziness (additive with metoprolol, sertraline). 71-year-old with polypharmacy — falls are a leading cause of morbidity.
Reassess pain control. Consider dose reduction or trial discontinuation. Physical therapy referral for osteoarthritis management.
Maximum PRN dose (2600 mg/day) exceeds safe threshold with warfarin. CKD 3a adds renal concern.
Restrict to ≤2000 mg/day with clear patient counseling. Consider scheduled low-dose regimen rather than PRN high-dose.

This patient has 5 different prescribers across Cardiology, PCP, Endocrinology, Psychiatry, and Rheumatology. Medication reconciliation conferences are strongly recommended to harmonize the treatment plan and address Major interactions.
Monitoring Plan: Weekly × 8 weeks, then every 2–4 weeks once stable
